title: Publish docs/ as VitePress site on GitHub Pages
description:
**Size:** 🔧 small
**Category:** docs
**Priority:** medium
**Source:** Proposed for 'The documentation under docs/ should be analogous to https'

Create a VitePress documentation site for the forge repository and deploy it to GitHub Pages. Involves 7 file operations:

1. **`package.json`** (root) — `name: "c5c3-forge-docs"`, VitePress `^1.6.3` devDependency, scripts: `docs:dev`/`docs:build`/`docs:preview`
2. **`docs/.vitepress/config.ts`** — Site config with `title: "CobaltCore Forge"`, `base: '/forge/'`, sidebar linking 5 existing reference docs (`keystone-crd`, `ci-workflow`, `build-images-workflow`, `container-images`, `kubernetes-packages`), GitHub social link to `c5c3/forge`
3. **`docs/.vitepress/theme/index.ts`** — Re-export DefaultTheme with custom.css import (identical to C5C3/C5C3 pattern)
4. **`docs/.vitepress/theme/custom.css`** — 1440px max-width, code block horizontal scroll, table overflow handling, sidebar width adjustments (identical to C5C3/C5C3)
5. **`docs/index.md`** — Landing page with hero section, project overview, link to architecture docs at `c5c3.github.io/C5C3`
6. **`.github/workflows/deploy-docs.yaml`** — Two-job workflow (build → deploy) following `github-actions-workflow-spdx-header-and-structure-convention` pattern exactly: SPDX header, quoted `"on"`, `permissions` block (contents: read, pages: write, id-token: write), concurrency group with cancel-in-progress on PR only. Triggers on push to main (paths: docs/**, package.json, package-lock.json, workflow file) + workflow_dispatch. Uses actions/checkout@v4, setup-node@v4 (Node 22), configure-pages@v5, upload-pages-artifact@v3, deploy-pages@v4
7. **`.gitignore`** (edit) — Add `node_modules/`, `docs/.vitepress/cache/`, `docs/.vitepress/dist/`

All new files must carry SPDX Apache-2.0 headers per `spdx-apache-20-header-on-infrastructure-files` pattern. Workflow must use `.yaml` extension per forge convention.

**Rationale:** The 5 reference docs under docs/reference/ are only accessible via GitHub's file browser, which lacks navigation, search, and readable formatting. Publishing as a VitePress site provides a professional documentation experience at c5c3.github.io/forge with sidebar navigation, consistent styling, and automatic deployment on push to main — matching the existing C5C3/C5C3 architecture docs site pattern.

**Affected Areas:**
- package.json
- docs/.vitepress/config.ts
- docs/.vitepress/theme/index.ts
- docs/.vitepress/theme/custom.css
- docs/index.md
- .github/workflows/deploy-docs.yaml
- .gitignore
elaboration:
**Size:** 🔧 small
**Category:** docs
**Priority:** medium

## Summary

Publish the `docs/` content from the forge repository as a VitePress static site on GitHub Pages. This involves creating a VitePress configuration (config, theme, landing page) analogous to the existing C5C3/C5C3 architecture docs site, adding a root-level `package.json` with VitePress dependency, and a GitHub Actions workflow that builds and deploys to GitHub Pages on pushes to `main`.

## Scope

**Included:**
- VitePress configuration at `docs/.vitepress/` (`config.ts`, `theme/index.ts`, `theme/custom.css`) — adapted from C5C3/C5C3 reference (same theme re-export, same custom CSS with 1440px width, code block and table overflow handling)
- Root-level `package.json` with VitePress `^1.6.3` dev dependency and `docs:dev`/`docs:build`/`docs:preview` scripts — mirrors C5C3/C5C3 `package.json` structure exactly
- Landing page at `docs/index.md` with hero section, project overview, and link to the architecture documentation site
- GitHub Actions workflow `.github/workflows/deploy-docs.yaml` (build + deploy to GitHub Pages), modeled from the reference `deploy-docs.yml` — includes `workflow_dispatch` trigger, `permissions` block (`contents: read`, `pages: write`, `id-token: write`), `concurrency` group, and the two-job pattern (`build` → `deploy`)
- Sidebar navigation covering existing `docs/reference/` content (5 files: `keystone-crd`, `ci-workflow`, `build-images-workflow`, `container-images`, `kubernetes-packages`)
- `.gitignore` update for `node_modules/`, `docs/.vitepress/cache/`, `docs/.vitepress/dist/`
- SPDX license headers on all new files (Apache-2.0, matching the reference repo convention)

**Excluded:**
- Migrating or duplicating architecture docs from `architecture/` submodule (separate site, already deployed via C5C3/C5C3)
- Writing new documentation content beyond the landing page (YAGNI)
- Custom VitePress plugins or search configuration (YAGNI)
- Custom domain configuration (infrastructure concern, separate from VitePress setup)
- npm lockfile generation (produced by `npm install`, not authored manually)

## Visualization

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    subgraph repo["forge repository"]
        pkg["package.json"]
        subgraph docs["docs/"]
            idx["index.md"]
            subgraph vp[".vitepress/"]
                cfg["config.ts"]
                subgraph theme["theme/"]
                    thIdx["index.ts"]
                    css["custom.css"]
                end
            end
            subgraph ref["reference/"]
                kc["keystone-crd.md"]
                ci["ci-workflow.md"]
                bi["build-images-workflow.md"]
                cimg["container-images.md"]
                kpkg["kubernetes-packages.md"]
            end
        end
        subgraph ghw[".github/workflows/"]
            deploy["deploy-docs.yaml"]
        end
    end

    subgraph pipeline["GitHub Actions Pipeline"]
        checkout["Checkout"] --> setup["Setup Node 22"]
        setup --> install["npm ci"]
        install --> build["npm run docs:build"]
        build --> pages["Configure Pages"]
        pages --> upload["Upload Pages Artifact"]
        upload --> dp["Deploy to GitHub Pages"]
    end

    deploy -.->|"triggers"| pipeline
    build -.->|"reads"| docs
    dp -.->|"publishes"| site["c5c3.github.io/forge"]
```

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant D as Developer
    participant GH as GitHub
    participant GA as GitHub Actions
    participant GP as GitHub Pages

    D->>GH: Push to main (docs/**)
    GH->>GA: Trigger deploy-docs workflow
    GA->>GA: Checkout + Setup Node 22
    GA->>GA: npm ci
    GA->>GA: npm run docs:build
    GA->>GA: Configure Pages + Upload artifact
    GA->>GP: Deploy Pages
    GP-->>D: Site live at c5c3.github.io/forge
```

## Key Components

- **`package.json`** (root) — Node.js project config with `name: "c5c3-forge-docs"`, VitePress `^1.6.3` dev dependency, `docs:dev`/`docs:build`/`docs:preview` scripts. Identical structure to `C5C3/C5C3/package.json`.
- **`docs/.vitepress/config.ts`** — VitePress site config: `title: "CobaltCore Forge"`, `base: '/forge/'`, sidebar with `Reference` section linking the 5 existing docs, GitHub social link to `c5c3/forge`.
- **`docs/.vitepress/theme/index.ts`** — Re-exports `DefaultTheme` with `custom.css` import. Identical pattern to C5C3/C5C3.
- **`docs/.vitepress/theme/custom.css`** — Wider content area (1440px `--vp-layout-max-width`), code block horizontal scrolling, table overflow handling, sidebar width adjustments. Identical to C5C3/C5C3.
- **`docs/index.md`** — VitePress landing page with hero section, brief project description, and external link to the architecture docs site (`c5c3.github.io/C5C3`).
- **`.github/workflows/deploy-docs.yaml`** — Two-job workflow (`build` → `deploy`). Triggered on push to `main` for `docs/**`, `package.json`, `package-lock.json`, workflow file, plus `workflow_dispatch`. Uses `actions/checkout@v4`, `actions/setup-node@v4` (Node 22), `actions/configure-pages@v5`, `actions/upload-pages-artifact@v3`, `actions/deploy-pages@v4`. Matches forge `.yaml` extension convention (vs. reference's `.yml`).
- **`.gitignore` update** — Add `node_modules/`, `docs/.vitepress/cache/`, `docs/.vitepress/dist/` entries to existing file.
